englishDefinitions: A humorous, often bawdy verse of five anapaestic lines, with the rhyme scheme aabba, and typically having an 8–8–5–5–8 cadence.
persianTranslation: یک بیت طنزآمیز و اغلب بداخلاق از پنج خط آناپستیک، با طرح قافیه aabba، و معمولاً دارای آهنگ 8-8-5-5-8 است.
examples:
The limerick, it would appear,
Is a verse form we owe Edward Lear;
Two long and two short
Lines rhymed, as was taught,
And a fifth just to bring up the rear.
